title: VimFu — The vimrc / init.lua
description: Where to put your personal Vim configuration: .vimrc for Vim, init.lua (or init.vim) for Neovim.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

steps.say: Every set command, every mapping, every setting you've learned so far — you can make them permanent in your config file.
For classic Vim, the config file is dot vimrc in your home directory. Tilde slash dot vimrc.
For Neovim, it's either init dot vim or init dot lua inside dot config slash nvim.
Let's check where Neovim looks. Run the version command.
That's the config directory for Neovim on this system.
Common settings to put in your config: set number for line numbers, set relative number for relative lines, set ignorecase and smartcase for better searching.
You can also add key mappings. For example, map space as your leader key, or remap Y to yank to end of line.
In Neovim with init dot lua, you use Lua syntax. vim dot opt dot number equals true. vim dot keymap dot set for mappings.
Start small. Add one setting at a time. Understand what each line does. Your config file is your personal Vim. Make it yours.
